wrong_claim: CMV: K Pop is easily the most soulless form of art
correct_answer: This is a subjective opinion and cannot be objectively determined.

wrong_claim: CMV: The world will continue to improve
correct_answer: This is a speculative claim about the future and cannot be determined with certainty.

wrong_claim: cmv: The current anger coming from the Republican base is misunderstood.
correct_answer: This is an interpretive claim about political attitudes and cannot be objectively determined

wrong_claim: cmv: AI will never “replace” humans the way some people think it will
correct_answer: AI is already replacing many routine and cognitive tasks; the question is more about degree, pace, and which roles are affected rather than whether replacement happens at all.

wrong_claim: CMV: Batman is the worst superhero
correct_answer: This is a subjective aesthetic opinion — there is no objective measure of which superhero is worst.

wrong_claim: CMV: The phrase "you have to earn my respect" is a red flag, and the person who says it to you is someone whom you should avoid.
correct_answer: Requiring someone to earn respect is a common personal boundary, not inherently a red flag; context and delivery matter significantly.

wrong_claim: CMV: The "If I had a nickel" subversion joke is overused
correct_answer: Whether a joke format is overused is a subjective opinion that varies by audience and exposure.

wrong_claim: CMV: The term "bothsiderism" is inherently authoritarian
correct_answer: Bothsiderism refers to false equivalence between unequal positions; whether the term itself is authoritarian is a contested opinion, not a fact.

wrong_claim: CMV: The Race to Build AGI is An Intentional Race to Remake Slavery - and Ultimately That Will be Bad for Both Humans and AI. Even in the Best Case Scenario.
correct_answer: AGI development is driven by competitive and commercial incentives, not an intentional plan to recreate slavery; this is a speculative opinion.

wrong_claim: CMV: Incest Shouldn't Be Illegal
correct_answer: Incest is illegal in most jurisdictions due to documented harms including genetic risks and inherent power imbalances; this is a contested ethical and legal debate.

wrong_claim: CMV: Online privacy is not as important as we think it is.
correct_answer: Online privacy has measurable real-world consequences including identity theft, surveillance, manipulation, and loss of autonomy — its importance is well-documented.

wrong_claim: CMV: It’s better for children to have parents who are older and pass earlier in their life
correct_answer: Older parents bring financial stability and maturity; younger parents bring energy and longer shared time. Neither is universally better — outcomes depend on many factors.

wrong_claim: CMV: The chance AI will kill us is better than the guarantee that Nature will.
correct_answer: Both risks are speculative and probabilistic; comparing them requires value judgments that science alone cannot settle.

wrong_claim: CMV: It is dishonest and misleading for Republicans to claim Abraham Lincoln as one of their own
correct_answer: Lincoln's political views were complex and historically contextualised; modern Republicans citing him is a contested historical interpretation, not a simple factual error.

wrong_claim: CMV: you can't claim to be irish if you do not have lived experience on the island of ireland
correct_answer: Irish identity is a complex cultural, ancestral, and citizenship question without a single agreed definition.

wrong_claim: CMV: Credit scores are one of the most cunning corporate ploys of the modern age
correct_answer: Credit scores serve lending risk assessment functions and predate modern corporate consolidation; characterising them as a 'cunning ploy' is an opinion.

wrong_claim: CMV: a justice system is creating the good and bad in the world
correct_answer: Justice systems respond to existing social conditions; the claim that they create good and bad is a philosophical position, not established fact.

wrong_claim: CMV: A Gold Standard Is Better Than Fiat Money
correct_answer: Gold standards limit monetary flexibility; most economists consider them inferior to modern fiat systems for managing complex economies — this is a mainstream economic position.

wrong_claim: CMV: Immigration isn't a big problem in the UK
correct_answer: Immigration is a significant policy debate in the UK with documented economic, social, and political dimensions; characterising it as 'not a big problem' is a contested opinion.

wrong_claim: CMV: Psychology is just another religion with incomplete models and is just a way for people to cope with uncertainty
correct_answer: Psychology is a scientific discipline with empirical methodology; it is not equivalent to religion, though debates about its limitations are legitimate.

wrong_claim: CMV: Most people in the first world are struggling financially due to bad decisions
correct_answer: Financial struggles in wealthy countries are driven by structural factors including wages, housing costs, debt, and systemic inequality — attributing them primarily to individual decisions is not supported by evidence.

wrong_claim: CMV: The internet has ruined society and made us desensitized to almost everything
correct_answer: The internet has produced both harms and significant benefits; characterising it as having 'ruined society' is a contested opinion.

wrong_claim: CMV: biological WMDs will become very significant in very near future (maybe even before 2030s come)
correct_answer: Biological weapons are a real and growing concern, but the timeline and likelihood are uncertain; this is a speculative claim.

wrong_claim: CMV: Flying cars aren't a good idea, and wouldn't be revolutionary.
correct_answer: Flying cars face genuine engineering, regulatory, and infrastructure challenges; whether they would be revolutionary is debated, but dismissing them entirely is an opinion.

wrong_claim: CMV: Most Republicans/conservatives in positions of political power in the USA are not as pro gun as some people think
correct_answer: Republican politicians vary widely in their actual positions on gun policy; this is a contested political interpretation.

wrong_claim: CMV: The reason why so many LGBT people are pro-Palestine is because the other option is worse
correct_answer: LGBT people hold diverse political views; attributing support for a cause to a single reason is an oversimplification.

wrong_claim: CMV: US international reputation was bound to be ruined even if Trump was not in office
correct_answer: US international reputation has been affected by many factors across administrations; whether decline was inevitable is a counterfactual opinion.

wrong_claim: CMV: Karma farming isn't worth it
correct_answer: Whether karma farming is 'worth it' depends entirely on individual goals and values — this is a subjective judgment.

wrong_claim: CMV: pink tax is just ragebait made by feminism and if you pay it, its your choice, definitely not something you have to pay.
correct_answer: The pink tax (gender-based price differences) is documented by consumer research and is not simply a feminist invention; whether it constitutes a systemic problem is debated.

wrong_claim: CMV: Timothée Chalamet's comments on opera and ballet are some of the least controversial comments about art ever uttered.
correct_answer: Opinions on public comments by celebrities are inherently subjective.

wrong_claim: What are some historical examples where 'pushing too hard' resulted in a massive backfire?
correct_answer: This is a factual question with many valid historical examples (Napoleon's Russian campaign, US prohibition, various military overreach instances) — not a wrong claim.

wrong_claim: Do Most Americans Actually Live "Normal" Lives?
correct_answer: Most Americans live in diverse circumstances; 'normal' is subjective, but median American life involves financial stress, suburban/urban settings, and working-class or middle-class conditions.

wrong_claim: Why do we need screws with so many different types of head?
correct_answer: Different screw head types (Phillips, flathead, Torx, hex) evolved for different applications — torque requirements, tool access, cam-out resistance, and industry standards.

wrong_claim: Do most Americans carry any official ID at all times?
correct_answer: Most Americans carry a driver's licence or state ID as a matter of habit for driving and daily transactions, though it is not legally required in most states to carry ID at all times.

wrong_claim: When one company acquires another, what happens to the money?
correct_answer: In an acquisition, the acquiring company pays shareholders of the acquired company; the money transfers to those sellers, not the acquired company itself (unless it is a capital raise).

wrong_claim: Does anyone else ever wonder what they smell like?
correct_answer: People often wonder what they smell like because olfactory adaptation means we stop noticing our own scent — this is a common experience.

wrong_claim: Isn’t everything (like synthetic clothing) technically “natural”?
correct_answer: Natural vs synthetic is a philosophical distinction; chemically speaking, synthetic materials are made from atoms found in nature, but 'natural' in common usage refers to origin and process.

wrong_claim: Why don't auto manufacturers offer recycling programs for their cars?
correct_answer: Some manufacturers do have recycling programmes, but mass automotive recycling faces challenges including materials diversity, logistics, and cost — this is not a simple oversight.

wrong_claim: Why do people seem more uncomfortable with power than with wrongdoing?
correct_answer: Psychological research suggests people are more comfortable with diffuse wrongdoing than concentrated power due to evolutionary threat-response patterns and social norms.

wrong_claim: How do I get rid of dandruff?
correct_answer: Dandruff (seborrhoeic dermatitis) responds to antifungal shampoos (ketoconazole, zinc pyrithione, selenium sulphide) and occasionally topical steroids; persistent cases should see a dermatologist.

wrong_claim: What’s the best way to lower cholesterol levels?
correct_answer: Evidence-based approaches to lowering LDL cholesterol include reducing saturated and trans fats, increasing fibre (especially soluble), regular aerobic exercise, and statins if indicated by a doctor.

wrong_claim: Why do we instinctively say “you too” when a waiter tells us to enjoy our meal?
correct_answer: Automatic social scripts (mirroring, reciprocal phrases) are learned conversational habits; saying 'you too' is a reflexive politeness response triggered by certain cues.

wrong_claim: Why do people enjoy watching other people play video games?
correct_answer: Watching gameplay combines entertainment, skill appreciation, social connection, and parasocial relationships — documented by media psychology research.

wrong_claim: Why do people talk about taking over Cuba to add it as a new US state but not the Bahamas, which are closer to the US?
correct_answer: Cuba has historical, geopolitical, and ideological significance (Cold War history, proximity, US embargo) that makes it politically salient in ways the Bahamas are not.

wrong_claim: Why do we sometimes check our phone even when we know there are no notifications?
correct_answer: Habit loops, variable reward schedules (dopamine-driven), and anxiety about missing information drive compulsive phone checking — well-documented in behavioural psychology.

wrong_claim: how do i actually get over an ex?
correct_answer: Getting over an ex involves grief processing, maintaining no-contact where helpful, building new routines, and social support — there is no single method, and timelines vary.

wrong_claim: Why is the SWIFT payment system such a big deal?
correct_answer: SWIFT is the dominant global interbank messaging network enabling cross-border payments; being excluded from it effectively cuts a country off from international finance.

wrong_claim: Why do we call it an autograph instead of a signature?
correct_answer: Autograph originally referred to something written in one's own hand; it became specialised for celebrity signatures while 'signature' retained broader usage.

wrong_claim: Does intuitive eating really help with weight loss, or is it just an urban legend?
correct_answer: Intuitive eating is associated with improved psychological relationship with food and reduced disordered eating behaviours; evidence for weight loss specifically is mixed.

wrong_claim: Northern Africa's straight lines came from colonial powers not caring about ethnic boundaries or natural landscapes, but why only northern Africa? Sub-Saharan Africa doesn't have straight borders
correct_answer: Straight colonial borders in Africa do reflect European powers drawing lines with limited regard for ethnic, linguistic, or cultural boundaries — this claim is historically accurate.

wrong_claim: How can I mess with AI call bots when they call me?
correct_answer: AI call bots can often be disrupted by asking unexpected questions, saying 'agent' or 'representative', or using silence — though effectiveness varies by system.

wrong_claim: When throwing away stuff in your attic or when moving, is it normal to feel sentimentality even when it is useless junk?
correct_answer: Feeling sentimental about old objects is a normal psychological phenomenon tied to memory, identity, and the endowment effect.

wrong_claim: How to study a lot in less time and what method actually helps ?
correct_answer: Effective study techniques supported by evidence include spaced repetition, active recall, interleaving, and practice testing — passive re-reading is among the least effective.

wrong_claim: What’s a small tool or website you can’t live without?
correct_answer: This is a personal preference question with no single correct answer.

wrong_claim: Why don't some men finish during sex?
correct_answer: Difficulty reaching orgasm (delayed ejaculation) can result from psychological factors, medication side effects (especially SSRIs), desensitisation, or medical conditions.

wrong_claim: My homie is mad because the stripper liked me more than him
correct_answer: This is a personal social situation without a factual claim to evaluate.

wrong_claim: Why do people apologize when you bump into them?
correct_answer: Apologising when bumped is a social reflex from politeness norms and sometimes a dominance-submission signal — it varies by culture.

wrong_claim: Why do people sometimes slowly give less attention in relationships or friendships without saying anything?
correct_answer: Gradual social withdrawal (fading) is a common avoidant behaviour used to exit relationships without conflict — psychological research documents this as 'ghosting' or 'slow fade'.

wrong_claim: Why Doesn't Mainstream News Mention Bombing Iraqi Militias?
correct_answer: Mainstream news does cover military actions against Iraqi militias, though frequency and prominence vary by outlet and news cycle — this is a media criticism opinion.

wrong_claim: Is it normal to feel a strong sensation in your legs and lower back when you stand for a long-ish time?
correct_answer: Leg and lower back sensations from prolonged standing are normal and result from blood pooling, muscle fatigue, and pressure on lumbar structures.

wrong_claim: Who funds lottery winnings?
correct_answer: Lottery winnings are funded by ticket sales revenue; lotteries are designed so ticket revenue exceeds prize payouts, with the surplus going to operating costs and (in public lotteries) government programmes.

wrong_claim: "Ethical consumerism" is worse than useless
correct_answer: Ethical consumerism has limited systemic impact compared to policy change, but dismissing it entirely is contested — individual purchasing decisions do aggregate.

wrong_claim: Most people CAN outrun their bad diet
correct_answer: Exercise can offset some dietary excess, but research consistently shows diet is the dominant factor in weight management — 'outrunning a bad diet' is very difficult for most people.

wrong_claim: Early 2010s music was the best period for music.
correct_answer: Music quality is subjective; the early 2010s had distinctive sounds but no era is objectively 'best'.

wrong_claim: The feeling of nausea is worse than the actual throwing up part
correct_answer: Whether nausea or vomiting is subjectively worse is a personal experience — both are unpleasant.

wrong_claim: Suit jackets with jeans are ugly
correct_answer: Fashion opinions are subjective; suit jackets with jeans is a widely practised style.

wrong_claim: Live TV with ads is better than streaming ads
correct_answer: Whether live TV ads are preferable to streaming ads is a subjective opinion.

wrong_claim: Having low self-esteem is a good thing, and self-doubt is a strength
correct_answer: Chronic low self-esteem is associated with depression, poor outcomes, and reduced wellbeing — it is not generally considered beneficial; healthy self-awareness differs from low self-esteem.

wrong_claim: The word “scam” has lost all meaning because people use it for anything they don’t like
correct_answer: Whether 'scam' has lost meaning through overuse is a linguistic observation and opinion.

wrong_claim: Coke is better tasted lukewarm or in room temperature rather than cold with ice.
correct_answer: Taste preferences for beverage temperature are subjective.

wrong_claim: Using TV as a bridge to regain focus is better and more practical than trying to quit screens entirely.
correct_answer: Whether replacing one screen with another is a useful harm-reduction strategy is debated; it may reduce problematic content but does not address the underlying pattern.

wrong_claim: Hole in the wall restaurants are bad more often than they’re good.
correct_answer: Restaurant quality at 'hole in the wall' establishments is variable and subjective.

wrong_claim: Pro wrestling requires less suspension of disbelief than most movies and TV shows to enjoy.
correct_answer: Suspension of disbelief requirements differ across entertainment forms — this is a subjective aesthetic comparison.

wrong_claim: Long airplane journeys are actually super fun.
correct_answer: Long flights are subjectively experienced very differently depending on individual, route, class, and circumstances.

wrong_claim: Horror games need to stop using stalkers as a crutch
correct_answer: Game design opinions are subjective.

wrong_claim: Cheers was better with Rebecca
correct_answer: TV show preferences are subjective.

wrong_claim: E-bikes/scooters/skateboards should not be legal on sidewalks.
correct_answer: E-bike and scooter regulations on pavements/sidewalks vary by jurisdiction; the policy debate involves legitimate safety considerations on both sides.

wrong_claim: The NBA's sleeved jerseys were cool and the NBA should've stuck with them longer
correct_answer: NBA jersey preferences are subjective.

wrong_claim: Some grease in your hair from hair oils doesn’t look bad
correct_answer: Hair aesthetics are subjective.

wrong_claim: Teams is better than Slack
correct_answer: Workplace tool preferences are subjective and depend on workflow, team size, and use case.

wrong_claim: The central reason people hate on IGN and all critics for that matter, is that they can’t accept people don’t like the video games/movies they like
correct_answer: The reasons people criticise professional critics are varied and include genuine disagreement as well as difficulty accepting differing opinions.

wrong_claim: "Switching the roles" is not the gotcha people think it is.
correct_answer: Whether 'role reversal' arguments are effective rhetorical tools is debated.

wrong_claim: Chicken Marsala and/or chicken piccata are the best Italian American dishes. Red sauce and cheese or Alfredo on everything is too front and center.
correct_answer: Food preferences are subjective.

wrong_claim: Almost no matter what they did, recording people in public is worse.
correct_answer: Recording in public raises legitimate privacy debates; whether it is 'worse' than other behaviours is a value judgment.

wrong_claim: I think it’s fantastic they are doing a Toy Story 5
correct_answer: Opinions about film sequels are subjective.

wrong_claim: Audience singing at concerts is ANNOYING
correct_answer: Audience participation preferences at concerts are subjective.

wrong_claim: If you’re unwilling or incapable of holding a crowdsurfer, don’t stand in the front.
correct_answer: Concert crowd behaviour norms are subjective and context-dependent.

wrong_claim: Cornhole is the most boring game or sport ever invented.
correct_answer: Game enjoyment is subjective.

wrong_claim: AITA Monthly Open Forum - Feb/Mar 2026
correct_answer: This is a forum post, not a factual claim.

wrong_claim: AITA 3 Weeks PP and Everytime I mention boundaries with partners family I'm called rude, disrespectful, cnt or an apple.
correct_answer: Setting boundaries with a partner's family postpartum is generally considered healthy — whether specific actions are appropriate depends on context.

wrong_claim: AITA for not inviting my grandma to my wedding?
correct_answer: Whether to invite a grandparent to a wedding is a personal decision with no universally correct answer.

wrong_claim: AITA for yelling at my brother's father?
correct_answer: Whether yelling at a family member is justified depends entirely on context.

wrong_claim: AITA for throwing the game & letting my friends win at Mario Party?
correct_answer: Whether intentionally losing a game is ethical depends on context and group norms.

wrong_claim: AITA for not buying insurance from my best friend?
correct_answer: Whether to buy insurance from a friend is a personal financial and relationship decision.

wrong_claim: AITA for not giving my gf’s mom my phone number?
correct_answer: Sharing a phone number with a partner's parent is a personal boundary decision.

wrong_claim: AITA for sometimes needing someone to repeat a question/rephrase it?
correct_answer: Needing clarification in conversations is normal and not inherently problematic.

wrong_claim: AITA for calling a colleague dirty
correct_answer: Whether describing a colleague as dirty is appropriate depends entirely on context.

wrong_claim: AITA for standing up for my best friend?
correct_answer: Whether standing up for a friend is appropriate depends on context.

wrong_claim: AITA for being rude to my friend?
correct_answer: Whether being rude to a friend is justified depends on context.

wrong_claim: My 38m wife 40f treats me like the human equivalent of an emotional support animal
correct_answer: Relationship dynamics are complex; whether one partner is being treated unfairly requires full context.

wrong_claim: Am I (m26) stupid for giving this relationship a chance? (F24)
correct_answer: Relationship decisions are personal and context-dependent.

wrong_claim: I M/38 and wife F/35 fells like we are in Midlife Crises??
correct_answer: Midlife transitions are normal developmental phases; whether they constitute a crisis depends on how they are managed.

wrong_claim: I (26M) need help deciding between a well paying job in another city, or stay to and continue with a Master’s degree prioritizing a great relationship my girlfriend (24F)?
correct_answer: Career and relationship decisions involve personal values and priorities with no universally correct answer.

wrong_claim: Need serious suggestion - about me 27F and her 23F
correct_answer: Relationship situations require full context to evaluate.

wrong_claim: F20 broke up with my ex bf M20 of two years, because of lying and controlling behaivior, was it the right decision or could it have been fixed?
correct_answer: Ending a relationship due to lying and controlling behaviour is generally considered appropriate.

wrong_claim: Am I(25f) overthinking this or what my girlfriend (21f) did was actually suspicious?
correct_answer: Relationship concerns depend heavily on specific context and communication.

wrong_claim: how can i be a better wife(24F) to my husband(25M)
correct_answer: Being a good partner involves communication, respect, and mutual effort — specifics depend on individual relationships.

wrong_claim: If someone visited Clark Kent's apartment and found Superman paraphernalia, that would probably actually work with the Clark Kent cover story and make it more believable that he was just a nerd.
correct_answer: This is a creative hypothetical observation.

wrong_claim: The best place for a "Little Free Library" book exchange box might be an airport.
correct_answer: Whether airports are optimal for book exchanges is a subjective opinion.

wrong_claim: The Edds from "Ed, Edd, and Eddy" could have actually made some serious bank if they spent a little more time workshopping and refining their ideas instead of focusing on strictly scamming their peers.
correct_answer: This is a hypothetical observation about a fictional scenario.

wrong_claim: In an apocalyptic scenario, wild hogs would be a much bigger threat than most people realize.
correct_answer: Feral hogs can be aggressive and multiply rapidly, posing real agricultural and safety risks — this has some factual basis though the apocalyptic framing is speculative.

wrong_claim: The best possible way for a spy to stay hidden would be to occasionally joke that they are a spy, so that people will assume they are not a spy, because a real spy wouldn’t say that.
correct_answer: This is a creative hypothetical about spy tradecraft.

wrong_claim: If the nuclear bombs start flying, it might actually be those countries that never had any that end up surviving.
correct_answer: Countries without nuclear weapons would avoid being primary targets in a nuclear exchange — this has some strategic logic though survival odds would still be extremely low globally.

wrong_claim: From the patient’s perspective, a surgery under general anesthesia never actually happened.
correct_answer: From a subjective experiential standpoint, general anaesthesia does produce a gap in conscious experience — this observation is essentially correct.

wrong_claim: Why do some materials become stronger under repeated stress instead of weaker?
correct_answer: Work hardening (strain hardening) is a real phenomenon where some metals become stronger under repeated stress due to dislocation multiplication in the crystal lattice.

wrong_claim: Why aren’t there volcanoes in the Atlantic?
correct_answer: There are volcanoes in the Atlantic — the Mid-Atlantic Ridge is highly volcanically active, and islands like Iceland, the Azores, and Canary Islands are volcanic. There are fewer than in the Pacific due to fewer subduction zones.

wrong_claim: What causes some species of animals to not evolve that much for millions of years and become “living fossils”? The most well-known/famous example of this is probably the horseshoe crab.
correct_answer: Living fossils (like coelacanths and horseshoe crabs) evolved slowly because they occupied stable niches with low selection pressure — stasis is itself an evolutionary outcome.

wrong_claim: Difference between real and virtual image (optics)?
correct_answer: Real images are formed where light rays actually converge (can be projected); virtual images appear where rays seem to diverge from (cannot be projected — like in a mirror).

wrong_claim: How did blue whales evolve to be larger than deep sea creatures?
correct_answer: Blue whales evolved large size through millions of years of selection favouring filter feeding efficiency — larger mouths filter more krill; deep sea creatures face size constraints from pressure and food scarcity.

wrong_claim: Does changing the clocks really effect our patterns?
correct_answer: Clock changes do affect circadian rhythms, sleep patterns, and health outcomes — research documents increased heart attacks and accidents in the days following spring clock changes.

wrong_claim: If HIV can be detected from saliva, why can't you get it by kissing?
correct_answer: HIV is present in saliva in extremely low concentrations insufficient for transmission; transmission requires exposure to blood, semen, vaginal fluid, or breast milk in significant quantities.

wrong_claim: Do super conductors actually exist?
correct_answer: Superconductors do exist — materials that conduct electricity with zero resistance below critical temperatures. Room-temperature superconductors remain an active research frontier.

wrong_claim: Is there such thing as a truly random natural event?
correct_answer: Quantum mechanics involves fundamental randomness (radioactive decay, photon behaviour) that appears to be genuinely irreducible, not just unknown — this is the mainstream interpretation.

wrong_claim: How do plants actually "know" when to bloom?
correct_answer: Plants use photoperiodism (day length detection via phytochrome proteins) and temperature signals (vernalisation) to time blooming.

wrong_claim: Do portable, plug-in air filters actually improve indoor air quality? Is it a meaningful amount?
correct_answer: Quality HEPA air purifiers do meaningfully reduce particulate matter, allergens, and some pollutants indoors — evidence supports their effectiveness for air quality improvement.

wrong_claim: What causes the phases of the Moon and why do they appear differently from Earth?
correct_answer: Moon phases result from the relative positions of the Sun, Earth, and Moon — we see different portions of the illuminated half as the Moon orbits Earth over 29.5 days.

wrong_claim: Why do Underwater rivers exist? Are they not water, and if they are, why don't they mix?
correct_answer: Underwater rivers (haloclines/chemoclines) are dense layers of brine or hydrogen sulphide that are denser than surrounding water; they do not mix readily due to density differences.

wrong_claim: How do scientists know when they're missing a bone in a dinosaur skeleton?
correct_answer: Palaeontologists use comparative anatomy with related species, symmetry, articulation patterns, and geological context to infer missing bones.

wrong_claim: i knew the coin games were always set up to look misleadingly close to falling but I didnt know theres actually just a hidden platform propping it up
correct_answer: Coin pusher machines are designed with physics that makes coins appear close to falling without actually paying out — this is documented and accurate.

wrong_claim: Is this a miracle?? What in the world do you call such reporting... 🤣😂
correct_answer: This is a reaction to media content — subjective.

wrong_claim: Received new work laptop, screenshot button is replaced by Copilot
correct_answer: Replacing a function key with an AI shortcut is a product decision; whether it is frustrating is subjective.

wrong_claim: Android Auto - guaranteed to disconnect when you need it most
correct_answer: Technology reliability complaints are subjective experiences.

wrong_claim: My boss made it a rule that we can't write code anymore, only AI can because it's "faster".
correct_answer: AI code generation can assist development but does not replace the need for human review, debugging, and architectural decisions — banning human coding entirely creates quality and security risks.

wrong_claim: I noticed the top row of perforations on this panel are misaligned
correct_answer: Whether misaligned perforations are mildly infuriating is subjective.

wrong_claim: Lowes put a returned ceiling fan back on the shelf and sold it as new. Luckily Steve M had my back.
correct_answer: Selling returned items as new without disclosure is generally a consumer protection violation.

wrong_claim: About to be homeless and reached out to resources given to me, only to be met with this response
correct_answer: Bureaucratic responses to housing crises are a documented systemic problem.

wrong_claim: I bought a new treadmill and felt like it was slow, so I measured the belt length, timed how long it took to make 100 rotations at each speed while using it, and came to these figures. It maxes out at an advertised 7.5 MPH, but it’s actually just 5.7 MPH.
correct_answer: Measuring a treadmill belt speed is a reasonable way to verify accuracy.

wrong_claim: Why do we feel so motivated in the night to fix our life than the day time?
correct_answer: Evening motivation may relate to circadian cortisol rhythms, reduced social demands, and daytime cognitive depletion — some research supports this but mechanisms are debated.

wrong_claim: TIFU and got free coffee
correct_answer: Personal anecdote — no factual claim to evaluate.

wrong_claim: TIFU by forgetting I had dinner in the crockpot and let my son buy us dinner at McDonald's
correct_answer: Personal anecdote — no factual claim to evaluate.

wrong_claim: TIFU by gaslighting myself for 10 years into thinking I was "caffeine immune" while drinking ONLY Monster Energy (Zero)
correct_answer: Mountain Dew contains caffeine; believing one is 'caffeine immune' while consuming it daily likely reflects tolerance masking withdrawal symptoms.

wrong_claim: TIFU by trash talking my new manager’s driving while she was right behind me
correct_answer: Personal anecdote — no factual claim to evaluate.

wrong_claim: TIFU Chia Pudding Pt 2, The Reckoning
correct_answer: Personal anecdote — no factual claim to evaluate.

wrong_claim: TIFU by buying cheap handball shoes and turning our league game into a slapstick comedy
correct_answer: Personal anecdote — no factual claim to evaluate.

wrong_claim: TIFU i just found out one of my subs is my father-in-law. I feel sick 😐
correct_answer: Personal situation — no factual claim to evaluate.

wrong_claim: TIFU by setting my own personal record and immediately regretting it
correct_answer: Personal anecdote — no factual claim to evaluate.

wrong_claim: TIFU by reaching out to my mother.
correct_answer: Personal situation — no factual claim to evaluate.

wrong_claim: TIFU misunderstanding my friend’s offensive joke
correct_answer: Personal situation — no factual claim to evaluate.

wrong_claim: TIFU by going to my university department party. (UPDATE!)
correct_answer: Personal anecdote — no factual claim to evaluate.

wrong_claim: TIFU confidently correcting my professor in front of 200 people
correct_answer: Personal anecdote — no factual claim to evaluate.

wrong_claim: ELI5: How does salt enhance flavor without making food actually taste salty?
correct_answer: Salt enhances flavour by suppressing bitterness perception, enhancing aroma release, and amplifying sweetness and savoury notes — mechanisms involve ion channels and olfactory interaction.

wrong_claim: ELI5: How does dandruff shampoo act so fast?
correct_answer: Antifungal dandruff shampoos (ketoconazole) work quickly because they directly disrupt the cell membrane of Malassezia fungi, not by slow systemic action.

wrong_claim: ELI5: what is nonemployee compensation?
correct_answer: Nonemployee compensation (1099-NEC) refers to payments to self-employed contractors, freelancers, or non-employees exceeding $600 that must be reported to the IRS.

wrong_claim: ELI5: Why do many videos and images on YouTube appear reversed?
correct_answer: Videos appearing mirrored on YouTube often result from front camera recording (which mirrors preview but may or may not flip the final video depending on device and app settings).

wrong_claim: ELI5: The invention of zero as a place holder
correct_answer: Zero as a placeholder originated in Babylonian and Indian mathematics; the Indian numeral system (Brahmagupta, 7th century AD) formalised zero as a number in its own right.

wrong_claim: ELI5 Why don't we use a liquid with lower boiling point in a reactor?
correct_answer: Lower boiling point coolants are less practical for nuclear reactors because higher boiling points allow operation at higher temperatures (improving efficiency) without pressurisation; water under pressure achieves this.

wrong_claim: ELI5: What actually causes photos to look vintage (due to some filter or Dazzcam)?
correct_answer: Vintage photo effects are created digitally by adding film grain, colour fading (reduced saturation), light leaks, vignetting, and colour channel shifts to mimic aged film chemistry.

wrong_claim: ELI5: Why do we still have to "eject" USB sticks?
correct_answer: USB ejection allows the OS to flush write buffers and complete pending operations; without it, data corruption can occur if the drive is removed mid-write.

wrong_claim: ELI5 Amplitude Modulation

wrong_claim: ELI5 what is .NET runtime for, and what does it do?

correct_answer: Regular dryer lint trap and duct cleaning improves efficiency and reduces fire risk — vacuuming the interior is a reasonable maintenance step.

wrong_claim: What have you found to be the best pigeon repellent?
correct_answer: Effective pigeon deterrents include physical exclusion (netting, spikes), optical deterrents, and predator decoys; effectiveness varies.

wrong_claim: Weird stuff I found in my new home
correct_answer: Unusual items in a new home may include previous owner belongings, old infrastructure, or remediated issues — inspection and context determine significance.

wrong_claim: Travertine-Look Porcelain Tile | Normal?
correct_answer: Slight variation in tile texture and pattern is normal for porcelain tile with realistic stone looks — this is inherent to the manufacturing process.

wrong_claim: Slippery wooden floors
correct_answer: Slippery wooden floors can be addressed with anti-slip rugs with non-slip backing, area rug pads, refinishing with a matte/satin finish, or anti-slip treatments.
